proposed_description: Multi-word adverbial idioms that work as a single adverb: a poco a poco (little by little), di solito (usually), all'improvviso (suddenly), in fretta (in a hurry), per caso (by chance), di nuovo (again). Learned whole, not derived; several are false-friend or distractor-prone (attualmente vs 'actually' sits at the lexical edge of this).
rationale: NOT needed for this A2 pass; all 46 grammar and 16 translation items map to the six existing leaves. Raised as a forward proposal from the OIC exercise drill (topic_area adverbs_comparison), which surfaces a rich, teachable set of locuzioni avverbiali with built-in distractor pairs (per tempo / or ora / per ora; al dettaglio vs all'ingrosso; a squarciagola, alla rinfusa, a bizzeffe, di malavoglia). These have no home in the current tree. If Architecture accepts it, it may want sub-leaves (temporal / manner / quantity phrases) rather than a single flat leaf; flagging rather than pre-deciding, since leaf shape is architecture-owned.
